chapter_title.en: Chapter on Prohibition of Shedding Blood and Taking Wealth Unlawfully, the Prohibition of Engaging in the Impermissible, and Repentance From Killing Whether Intentional or Accidental

text: 5153 - وَ رَوَى هِشَامُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "لاَ يَزَالُ اَلْمُؤْمِنُ فِي فُسْحَةٍ مِنْ دِينِهِ مَا لَمْ يُصِبْ دَماً حَرَاماً" وَ قَالَ "لاَ يُوَفَّقُ قَاتِلُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِ مُتَعَمِّداً لِلتَّوْبَةِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5153 - Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"A believer remains within the bounds of his faith as long as he does not commit unlawful killing."
Imam (as) also said: "One who intentionally kills a believer is not granted success in repentance."
